purpose: Empirically validate hexa-runner warm-exec ~50ms / cold-start ~5s claims from omega_hexa_4_docker_hard_landing.json

bench_script.method: python3 time.time()*1000 wallclock around exec; sorted samples for p50/p95

scenarios_run.config: HEXA_RESOLVER_FORCE_DOWN=1 hexa /tmp/_b.hexa (full resolver path including probe + image_inspect + container_inspect + docker exec + hexa interp)
docker exec -i -w PWD -e HEXA_RESOLVER_NO_REROUTE=1 hexa-exec /usr/local/bin/hexa /tmp/_b.hexa (skip resolver, direct exec)
docker rm -f hexa-exec; HEXA_RESOLVER_FORCE_DOWN=1 hexa /tmp/_b.hexa (force fresh container creation + first exec)
docker exec -i hexa-exec /bin/true (isolate pure docker exec overhead, no hexa)
docker exec -i hexa-exec /usr/local/bin/hexa --version (hexa cold-startup, no script parse)

claim_validation.warm_exec_verdict: UNDERSTATED — actual is 2.06x cited (103ms vs 50ms claim) for raw docker-exec path; 4.9x (245ms) when including resolver probe overhead
claim_validation.cold_start_verdict: OVERSTATED by 8.7x — cold container recreate measured 575ms mean (range 545-605ms), well under cited 5s. Claim was likely a worst-case reasoning estimate, not measurement.
claim_validation.summary: Cited figures were directional but inaccurate: warm understated 2x, cold overstated ~9x. Docker exec on Darwin/Docker-Desktop is faster than feared but slower than ideal.

dominant_components_in_warm_exec.hexa_binary_startup_note: negligible — `hexa --version` runs in 98ms vs `/bin/true` 103ms; binary load is essentially free on top of docker-exec
dominant_components_in_warm_exec.resolver_overhead_breakdown: docker_info + image_inspect + container_inspect + env_hash + connectivity_probe ~= 142ms when not cached; HEXA_ROUTING_CACHE=1 with 5s TTL would eliminate most
dominant_components_in_warm_exec.verdict: Pure docker-exec round-trip is THE dominant cost (~103ms = 59% of warm path). Hexa script parse adds 73ms. Resolver adds another 142ms unless cached.

recommendation_if_warm_overhead_too_high.risk: low — cache is env_hash invalidated, 5s TTL bounds staleness, fail-loud on stale-route
recommendation_if_warm_overhead_too_high.effort: trivial — flip HEXA_ROUTING_CACHE default from 0 to 1

recommendation_if_warm_overhead_too_high.approach: long-lived hexa daemon inside container reading scripts over unix socket; bypass docker exec setup per call
recommendation_if_warm_overhead_too_high.risk: medium — adds daemon lifecycle, stdin/stdout pipelining, signal handling

recommendation_if_warm_overhead_too_high.approach: skip docker entirely on Darwin via signed darwin-bypass marker (already partially scaffolded — HEXA_RESOLVER_NO_DARWIN_BYPASS env)
recommendation_if_warm_overhead_too_high.risk: medium-high — loses container isolation guarantees; only viable when caller is already trusted
recommendation_if_warm_overhead_too_high.effort: low — wiring already exists, just default policy

rollback.side_effects: none — bench is read-only on infra; container hexa-exec was rm/recreated during cold-recreate samples but resolver auto-recreates on next call

next_cycles_suggested: omega_runner_6: enable HEXA_ROUTING_CACHE default-on + measure warm-resolver delta (predict 245ms→103ms)
omega_runner_7: investigate persistent-socket exec daemon if 100ms still too high for hot loops
